title: September is the best time to visit Beijing

vocabulary.hint: Character Breakdown: 旅 -> 方 (square) + 丿 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 4)) + 一 (one) + 氏 (clan) | 游 -> 氵 (water) + 方 (square) + 丿 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 4)) + 一 (one) + 子 (child)
Character Breakdown: 觉 -> ⺍ + 冖 ("cover" radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 14), occurring in 军, 写, 冠 etc) + 见 (to see) | 得 -> 彳 (step) + 日 (sun) + 一 (one) + 寸 (a unit of length)
Character Breakdown: 最 -> 曰 (to speak) + 耳 (ear) + 又 ((once) again)
Character Breakdown: 为 -> 力 (surname Li) + ⺀ | 什 -> 亻 (person) + 十 (ten) | 么 -> 丿 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 4)) + 厶
Character Breakdown: 也 -> 乚 (component in Chinese characters) + ㇆ + 丨 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 2))
Character Breakdown: 运 -> 辶 (movement) + 二 (two) + 厶 | 动 -> 二 (two) + 厶 + 力 (surname Li)
Character Breakdown: 踢 -> ⻊ + 日 (sun) + 勹 (archaic variant of 包[bao1]) + No glyph available | 足 (foot) | 球 -> 王 (surname Wang) + 𠂇 + 丶 (see 點|点[dian3]) + 氺 (archaic variant of 水[shui3])
Character Breakdown: 一 (one) | 起 -> 走 (walk) + 己 (self)
Character Breakdown: 要 -> 覀 + 女 (woman)
Character Breakdown: 新 -> 立 (stand) + 十 (ten) + 小 (small) + 斤 (axe)
Character Breakdown: 它 -> 宀 (roof) + 匕 (dagger)
Character Breakdown: 眼 -> 目 (eye) + 艮 (mountain) | 睛 -> 目 (eye) + 青 (blue)

grammar.explanation: 要 is used before a verb to indicate a desire or necessity ('to want to', 'need to').

examples.pinyin: Wǒ yào qù Běijīng lǚyóu.
examples.english: I want to go to Beijing to travel.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ yào hē chá ma?
examples.english: Do you want to drink tea?

examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yào mǎi ge xīn diànnǎo.
examples.english: We need to buy a new computer.

grammar.explanation: 最 is used before an adjective or a psychological verb to indicate the superlative degree ('most').

examples.pinyin: Běijīng jiǔyuè tiānqì zuì hǎo.
examples.english: The weather in Beijing is the best in September.

examples.pinyin: Tā zuì xǐhuan tī zúqiú.
examples.english: He likes playing football the most.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ juéde shéi zuì piàoliang?
examples.english: Who do you think is the most beautiful?

title: I get up at six every day

vocabulary.hint: Character Breakdown: 生 (life) | 病 -> 疒 (sickness) + 一 (one) + 人 (person) + 冂 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 13), occurring in 用, 同, 网 etc)
Character Breakdown: 每 -> 丿 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 4)) + 一 (one) + 母 (female)
Character Breakdown: 早 -> 日 (sun) + 十 (ten) | 上 -> ⺊ + 一 (one)

Character Breakdown: 跑 -> ⻊ + 勹 (archaic variant of 包[bao1]) + 巳 (6th earthly branch: 9-11 a.m., 4th solar month (5th May-5th June), year of the Snake) | 步 -> 止 (stop) + 小 (small) + 丿 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 4))
Character Breakdown: 起 -> 走 (walk) + 己 (self) | 床 -> 广 (surname Guang) + 木 (wood)
Character Breakdown: 药 -> 艹 (grass) + 纟 (silk) + 勹 (archaic variant of 包[bao1]) + 丶 (see 點|点[dian3])
Character Breakdown: 身 (body) | 体 -> 亻 (person) + 木 (wood) + 一 (one)
Character Breakdown: 出 (to go out) | 院 -> 阝 (city/mound) + 宀 (roof) + 二 (two) + 儿 (son)
Character Breakdown: 高 (high)
Character Breakdown: 米 (rice)
Character Breakdown: 知 -> 矢 (arrow) + 口 (mouth) | 道 -> 辶 (movement) + 首 (head)
Character Breakdown: 休 -> 亻 (person) + 木 (wood) | 息 -> 自 (self) + 心 (heart)
Character Breakdown: 忙 -> 忄 + 匸 ("cover" or "conceal" radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 23)) + 丶 (see 點|点[dian3])
Character Breakdown: 时 -> 日 (sun) + 寸 (a unit of length) | 间 -> 门 (door) + 日 (sun)

grammar.explanation: 每 means 'every' or 'each'. It is often followed by a measure word and a noun. It often works with the adverb 都 (dōu).

examples.pinyin: Wǒ měitiān liù diǎn qǐchuáng.
examples.english: I get up at six every day.

examples.pinyin: Měi ge xuésheng dōu hěn nǔlì.
examples.english: Every student is very hard-working.

examples.pinyin: Tā měinián qù yí cì Zhōngguó.
examples.english: He goes to China once every year.

grammar.explanation: Used to ask about the degree of a quality, such as height, age, or distance.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ duō gāo?
examples.english: How tall are you?

examples.pinyin: Nǐ érzi duō dà le?
examples.english: How old is your son?

examples.pinyin: Cóng zhèr dào nàr yǒu duō yuǎn?
examples.english: How far is it from here to there?

vocabulary.hint: Character Breakdown: 手 (hand) | 表 -> 龶 + ㇒ + ㇗ + No glyph available
Character Breakdown: 千 -> ㇒ + 十 (ten)
Character Breakdown: 报 -> 扌 + 卩 + 又 ((once) again) | 纸 -> 纟 (silk) + 氏 (clan)
Character Breakdown: 送 -> 辶 (movement) + 丷 ("eight" component in Chinese characters) + 一 (one) + 大 (big)
Character Breakdown: 一 (one) | 下 -> 一 (one) + 卜 (surname Bu)
Character Breakdown: 牛 (cow) | 奶 -> 女 (woman) + ㇎ + 丿 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 4))
Character Breakdown: 房 -> 户 (a household) + 方 (square) | 间 -> 门 (door) + 日 (sun)
Character Breakdown: 丈 -> 十 (ten) + 乂 (to regulate) | 夫 -> 人 (person) + 二 (two)

Character Breakdown: 旁 -> 亠 + 丷 ("eight" component in Chinese characters) + 冖 ("cover" radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 14), occurring in 军, 写, 冠 etc) + 方 (square) | 边 -> 辶 (movement) + 力 (surname Li)
Character Breakdown: 真 -> 十 (ten) + 目 (eye) + 一 (one) + 八 (eight)
Character Breakdown: 粉 -> 米 (rice) + 八 (eight) + 刀 (surname Dao) | 色 (color)
Character Breakdown: 颜 -> 立 (stand) + 厂 ("cliff" radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 27), occurring in 原, 历, 压 etc) + 彡 + 页 (page) | 色 (color)
Character Breakdown: 左 -> 𠂇 + 工 (work) | 边 -> 辶 (movement) + 力 (surname Li)
Character Breakdown: 红 -> 纟 (silk) + 工 (work) | 色 (color)

grammar.explanation: A '的' phrase is formed by adding '的' to a noun, pronoun, or adjective. It functions as a noun, often allowing you to omit the noun if it is clear from context.

examples.pinyin: Zhè kuài shǒubiǎo shì wǒ de.
examples.english: This watch is mine.

examples.chinese: 红的是我的，白的是他的。
examples.pinyin: Hóng de shì wǒ de, bái de shì tā de.
examples.english: The red one is mine, the white one is his.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ yào mǎi jiàn piányi de.
examples.english: I want to buy a cheap one.

grammar.explanation: Used after a verb to indicate that the action is short, brief, or a trial attempt. It makes the tone softer and more polite.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ kàn yíxià.
examples.english: Have a look.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ xiūxi yíxià.
examples.english: I will take a short rest.

examples.pinyin: Qǐng děng yíxià.
examples.english: Please wait a moment.

title: He recommended me for this job

vocabulary.hint: Character Breakdown: 生 (life) | 日 (sun)

Character Breakdown: 接 -> 扌 + 立 (stand) + 女 (woman)
Character Breakdown: 晚 -> 日 (sun) + ⺈ + 口 (mouth) + 丨 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 2)) + 乚 (component in Chinese characters) | 上 -> ⺊ + 一 (one)
Character Breakdown: 问 -> 门 (door) + 口 (mouth)
Character Breakdown: 非 (wrong) | 常 -> ⺌ + 冖 ("cover" radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 14), occurring in 军, 写, 冠 etc) + 口 (mouth) + 巾 (cloth)
Character Breakdown: 开 -> 一 (one) + 廾 (hands joined) | 始 -> 女 (woman) + 厶 + 口 (mouth)
Character Breakdown: 已 (already) | 经 -> 纟 (silk) + 龴 + 工 (work)
Character Breakdown: 介 -> 人 (person) + No glyph available | 绍 -> 纟 (silk) + 刀 (surname Dao) + 口 (mouth)
Character Breakdown: 帮 -> 一 (one) + 二 (two) + 丨 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 2)) + 阝 (city/mound) + 巾 (cloth)
Character Breakdown: 长 (long)

grammar.explanation: Used to emphasize the person (agent) who performed a specific past action.

examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū shì gēge mǎi de.
examples.english: This book was bought by my older brother.

examples.pinyin: Zhège gōngzuò shì tā jièshào de.
examples.english: This job was recommended by him.

examples.pinyin: Nà shì Zhāng lǎoshī xiě de.
examples.english: That was written by Teacher Zhang.

grammar.explanation: Used to indicate that an action has already taken place or a state has reached a certain point. Usually used with 了 at the end.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ yǐjīng chī yào le.
examples.english: I have already taken the medicine.

examples.pinyin: Tā yǐjīng chūyuàn le.
examples.english: He has already left the hospital.

examples.pinyin: Yǐjīng shí diǎn le, kuài shuìjiào ba.
examples.english: It's already 10 o'clock, go to sleep quickly.

vocabulary.hint: Character Breakdown: 准 -> 冫 ("ice" radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 15), occurring in 冰, 决, 次) + 隹 (bird) | 备 -> 夂 ("walk slowly" component in Chinese characters) + 田 (field)
Character Breakdown: 就 -> 亠 + 口 (mouth) + 小 (small) + 尢 (lame) + 丶 (see 點|点[dian3])

grammar.explanation: Used to indicate a conclusion or resolution reached naturally based on what has been previously discussed ('then', 'just').

examples.pinyin: Nǐ jiù mǎi zhè jiàn ba.
examples.english: Then just buy this one.

examples.pinyin: Tā shuō qù, wǒ jiù qù.
examples.english: If he says he's going, then I'll go.

examples.chinese: 明天是星期六，我就不看书了。
examples.pinyin: Míngtiān shì xīngqīliù, wǒ jiù bú kàn shū le.
examples.english: Tomorrow is Saturday, so I just won't study.

grammar.explanation: Basic structure: A + 比 + B + Adjective. It indicates that A possesses a higher degree of a quality than B.

examples.pinyin: Jīntiān bǐ zuótiān rè.
examples.english: Today is hotter than yesterday.

examples.pinyin: Tā bǐ wǒ gāo.
examples.english: He is taller than me.

examples.pinyin: Zhè jiàn yīfu bǐ nà jiàn guì.
examples.english: This piece of clothing is more expensive than that one.

grammar.explanation: Used to indicate a range in time or space ('from... to...').

examples.pinyin: cóng Běijīng dào Shànghǎi
examples.english: from Beijing to Shanghai

examples.chinese: 我从八点到十点在工作。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ cóng bā diǎn dào shí diǎn zài gōngzuò.
examples.english: I am working from 8:00 to 10:00.

examples.pinyin: Cóng wǒ jiā dào xuéxiào hěn jìn.
examples.english: From my home to school is very close.

grammar.explanation: Used to indicate the distance between two places or two points in time.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ jiā lí xuéxiào hěn jìn.
examples.english: My home is very close to the school.

examples.pinyin: Gōngsī lí jīchǎng yuǎn bù yuǎn?
examples.english: Is the company far from the airport?

examples.chinese: 离我的生日还有一个星期。
examples.pinyin: Lí wǒ de shēngrì hái yǒu yí ge xīngqī.
examples.english: It is still one week away from my birthday.

grammar.explanation: Attached to a verb to indicate that a state is continuing or a situation remains unchanged.

examples.pinyin: Mén kāi zhe ne.
examples.english: The door is open.

examples.pinyin: Tā chuān zhe yí jiàn hóng yīfu.
examples.english: He is wearing a red piece of clothing.

examples.pinyin: shǒu li ná zhe bǐ
examples.english: holding a pen in hand

grammar.point: 也 (yě) and 都 (dōu) for Total Negation
grammar.explanation: Used with '一 + Measure Word + Noun' to indicate that nothing or nobody fits the description ('not at all', 'not even one').

examples.pinyin: Wǒ yí ge píngguǒ yě bù xiǎng chī.
examples.english: I don't want to eat even one apple.

examples.pinyin: Tā yìdiǎnr shuǐ dōu méi hē.
examples.english: He didn't drink even a little water.

examples.pinyin: Jīntiān yí ge rén dōu méi lái.
examples.english: Not even one person came today.

title: There were too many questions; I didn't finish

grammar.point: Result Complements (完, 懂, 对, 好)
grammar.explanation: A verb or adjective used after another verb to indicate the outcome or result of the action.

examples.pinyin: Tí wǒ zuò wán le.
examples.english: I have finished doing the questions.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ kàn dǒng le ma?
examples.english: Did you understand (what you read)?

examples.pinyin: Nǐ shuō duì le.
examples.english: You said it correctly.

grammar.explanation: 就 indicates that an action happens early, fast, or smoothly. 才 indicates that it happens late, slow, or with difficulty.

examples.pinyin: Tā bā diǎn jiù lái le.
examples.english: He came as early as 8:00.

examples.pinyin: Tā bā diǎn cái lái.
examples.english: He didn't come until 8:00.

examples.chinese: 坐飞机一个小时就到了。
examples.pinyin: Zuò fēijī yí ge xiǎoshí jiù dào le.
examples.english: It only takes one hour to arrive by plane.

Character Breakdown: 呢 -> 口 (mouth) + 尸 (person representing the dead (during burial ceremonies)) + 匕 (dagger)

Character Breakdown: 课 -> 讠 (speech) + 田 (field) + 木 (wood)

grammar.explanation: Used after a verb to indicate that an action has been experienced in the past ('have done').

examples.pinyin: Wǒ jiàn guo tā.
examples.english: I have seen him before.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ qù guo Zhōngguó ma?
examples.english: Have you been to China?

examples.pinyin: Wǒ méi kàn guo zhège diànyǐng.
examples.english: I haven't seen this movie.

title: He is three years older than me

vocabulary.hint: Character Breakdown: 唱 -> 口 (mouth) + 昌 (surname Chang) | 歌 -> 哥 (elder brother) + 欠 (deficient)
Character Breakdown: 男 -> 田 (field) + 力 (surname Li)
Character Breakdown: 女 (woman)

Character Breakdown: 右 -> 𠂇 + 口 (mouth) | 边 -> 辶 (movement) + 力 (surname Li)
Character Breakdown: 比 (compare)
Character Breakdown: 便 -> 亻 (person) + 一 (one) + 日 (sun) + 丨 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 2)) + 乂 (to regulate) | 宜 -> 宀 (roof) + 月 (meat) + 一 (one)
Character Breakdown: 说 -> 讠 (speech) + 丷 ("eight" component in Chinese characters) + 口 (mouth) + 儿 (son) | 话 -> 讠 (speech) + 舌 (tongue)
Character Breakdown: 可 -> 一 (one) + 亅 ("vertical stroke with hook" radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 6)) + 口 (mouth) | 能 -> 厶 + 月 (meat) + No glyph available
Character Breakdown: 去 -> 土 (earth) + 厶 | 年 -> 丿 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 4)) + 一 (one) + 十 (ten) + ㇗ + 丨 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 2))
Character Breakdown: 姓 -> 女 (woman) + 生 (life)

grammar.explanation: A + 比 + B + Adjective + Complement. The complement can be '一点儿' (a bit), '一些' (a few), '多了' (a lot), or '得多' (much more).

examples.pinyin: Tā bǐ wǒ dà sān suì.
examples.english: He is three years older than me.

examples.pinyin: Jīntiān bǐ zuótiān lěng yìdiǎnr.
examples.english: Today is a bit colder than yesterday.

examples.pinyin: Shùxué bǐ lìshǐ nán duō le.
examples.english: Maths is much harder than history.

vocabulary.hint: Character Breakdown: 下 -> 一 (one) + 卜 (surname Bu) | 雪 -> 雨 (rain) + 彐
Character Breakdown: 零 -> 雨 (rain) + 人 (person) + 丶 (see 點|点[dian3]) + 龴
Character Breakdown: 度 -> 广 (surname Guang) + 廿 (twenty (20), in a limited number of set expressions) + 又 ((once) again)
Character Breakdown: 穿 -> 穴 (cave) + 牙 (tooth)
Character Breakdown: 进 -> 辶 (movement) + No glyph available + 二 (two)

Character Breakdown: 弟 -> 丷 ("eight" component in Chinese characters) + 弓 (surname Gong) + 丨 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 2)) + ㇒ | 弟 -> 丷 ("eight" component in Chinese characters) + 弓 (surname Gong) + 丨 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 2)) + ㇒
Character Breakdown: 近 -> 辶 (movement) + 斤 (axe)

grammar.explanation: Indicates that an action is about to happen very soon ('about to'). Variants: 快要...了, 就要...了.

examples.pinyin: Yào xià yǔ le.
examples.english: It is about to rain.

examples.pinyin: Huǒchē jiùyào kāi le.
examples.english: The train is about to leave.

examples.pinyin: Xīn de yì nián kuàiyào dào le.
examples.english: The new year is coming soon.

vocabulary.hint: Character Breakdown: 着 -> 羊 (surname Yang) + 目 (eye)
Character Breakdown: 手 (hand)
Character Breakdown: 拿 -> 人 (person) + 一 (one) + 口 (mouth) + 手 (hand)
Character Breakdown: 铅 -> 钅 (gold/metal) + 几 (small table) + 口 (mouth) | 笔 -> ⺮ + 毛 (hair)
Character Breakdown: 班 -> 玨 (gems mounted together) + 丿 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 4)) + 丶 (see 點|点[dian3])
Character Breakdown: 长 (long)
Character Breakdown: 笑 -> ⺮ + ㇒ + 大 (big)
Character Breakdown: 宾 -> 宀 (roof) + 兵 (soldiers) | 馆 -> 饣 (eat) + 宀 (roof) + 㠯
Character Breakdown: 一 (one) | 直 -> 十 (ten) + 目 (eye) + 一 (one)
Character Breakdown: 往 -> 彳 (step) + 王 (surname Wang) + 丶 (see 點|点[dian3])
Character Breakdown: 路 -> ⻊ + 夂 ("walk slowly" component in Chinese characters) + 口 (mouth) | 口 (mouth)

grammar.explanation: Subject + 把 + Object + Verb + Other elements. Used to describe how an object is handled or affected by an action (usually results in displacement).

examples.pinyin: Wǒ bǎ shū fàng zài zhuōzi shang le.
examples.english: I put the book on the table.

examples.pinyin: Qǐng bǎ kōngtiáo guān le.
examples.english: Please turn off the air conditioner.

examples.pinyin: Tā bǎ bǐ nòng huài le.
examples.english: He broke the pen.

vocabulary.hint: Character Breakdown: 有 -> 𠂇 + 月 (meat) | 意 -> 音 (sound) + 心 (heart) | 思 -> 田 (field) + 心 (heart)
Character Breakdown: 虽 -> 口 (mouth) + 虫 (insect) | 然 -> 月 (meat) + 犬 (dog) + 灬
Character Breakdown: 但 -> 亻 (person) + 日 (sun) + 一 (one) | 是 -> 日 (sun) + 一 (one) + 龰
Character Breakdown: 次 -> 冫 ("ice" radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 15), occurring in 冰, 决, 次) + 欠 (deficient)
Character Breakdown: 玩 -> 王 (surname Wang) + 二 (two) + 儿 (son) | 儿 (son)
Character Breakdown: 晴 -> 日 (sun) + 青 (blue)
Character Breakdown: 百 -> 一 (one) + 白 (white)

grammar.explanation: Object + 被 + Subject + Verb + Other elements. Indicates that the object has undergone an action ('was done by').

examples.pinyin: Dàngāo bèi dìdi chī le.
examples.english: The cake was eaten by my younger brother.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ de shǒujī bèi tā ná zǒu le.
examples.english: My phone was taken away by him.

examples.pinyin: Yīfu bèi xǐ gānjìng le.
examples.english: The clothes were washed clean.

vocabulary.hint: Character Breakdown: 新 -> 立 (stand) + 十 (ten) + 小 (small) + 斤 (axe) | 年 -> 丿 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 4)) + 一 (one) + 十 (ten) + ㇗ + 丨 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 2))
Character Breakdown: 票 -> 覀 + 示 (spirit)
Character Breakdown: 火 (fire) | 车 (cart) | 站 -> 立 (stand) + ⺊ + 口 (mouth)
Character Breakdown: 大 (big) | 家 -> 宀 (roof) + 豕 (pig)
Character Breakdown: 更 -> 一 (one) + 日 (sun) + 丨 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 2)) + 乂 (to regulate)
Character Breakdown: 妹 -> 女 (woman) + 木 (wood) + 一 (one) | 妹 -> 女 (woman) + 木 (wood) + 一 (one)
Character Breakdown: 阴 -> 阝 (city/mound) + 月 (meat)

grammar.explanation: Repeating a verb indicates that the action is short, quick, casual, or an attempt. For single-syllable verbs: AA (e.g. 看看). For double-syllable verbs: ABAB (e.g. 休息休息).

examples.pinyin: xiūxi xiūxi
examples.english: to have a short rest

examples.pinyin: Wǒ xiǎng kànkan zhè běn shū.
examples.english: I want to have a look at this book.

examples.pinyin: Bāng wǒ zhǔnbèi zhǔnbèi.
examples.english: Help me prepare a bit.
